title: mcp-stage stage definition (v1)
description: One declarative document = a whole stage: upstreams, fixtures, scenario overlays, assertions, seed, clock, ttl. YAML or JSON on disk; this schema validates the parsed object. Structurally multi-upstream (PRD §12.3): `upstreams` is an array and every tool reference is upstream-qualified. The v1 runtime rejects >1 upstream at load time — the FORMAT does not.

seed.description: Seeds the deterministic ID generators and any future stochastic behavior. Same definition + same seed = identical world, identical generated entity IDs across runs (PRD §12.5).

ttl.description: Auto-cleanup horizon for API-created stages, e.g. '2h'. Ignored for CLI-run stages.

clock.description: Virtual clock (PRD §12.1). Relative timestamps in fixtures ('-2d', '+3h') resolve against `start` at seed time. Each session gets its own clock instance; stage_advance_time / the control API move it.

start.description: 'now' or an ISO 8601 datetime. 'now' is captured once at session creation, then the clock is virtual.

toolRef.description: Upstream-qualified tool name: '<upstream_id>:<tool>', e.g. 'linear:create_issue'. The upstream prefix is optional while exactly one upstream exists, but the qualified form is always valid — definitions written today survive multi-upstream.

mode.description: LIVE = passthrough (+ optional record). STAGED = replay or fork (PRD §12: two modes; replay/fork are the two STAGED backends).

auth.description: OAuth/bearer passthrough config (PRD §12.8). 'passthrough' forwards the client's Authorization header untouched. 'static' injects a token from env — for CI where the harness holds credentials.

token_env.description: Env var name when strategy=static. Never a literal token in the definition.

cassette.description: Output path for the JSONL cassette.

match.description: exact = full params deep-equal in recorded order. tool_args = tool name + normalized args, order-free.

mapping.description: Path to the tool-semantics mapping file (reads/writes/entity keys) for this upstream.

fixtures.description: Fixture files (YAML/JSON) seeding the shadow store. Applied in order; later files may reference entities from earlier ones.

unmapped_tool.description: Behavior when the agent calls a tool the mapping doesn't declare. Never passthrough — fork must not touch production.

override.description: Scenario overlay applied before mode handling — works on LIVE and STAGED alike (PRD §12: cross-cutting).

args.description: Partial deep match against tool arguments. Present keys must match; absent keys are ignored.

calls.description: Which invocations of this tool (1-based, counted per session): '3' (only the 3rd), '3..' (3rd onward), '1..2', '*' (all).

oneOf.description: Tool-shaped empty success: empty content/results per the tool's output shape.

payload.description: Served verbatim as the result. Default: a structurally plausible but schema-violating body.

delay_ms.description: Latency injection. Composable: set delay_ms with any behavior, or alone with no `behavior` sibling — hence behavior is required only when delay_ms is absent (enforced at load, not in-schema, to keep this readable).

args.description: At least `min` calls must partially match these args.
If set, only calls matching these args violate the assertion.

oneOf.description: Every call to `after` must be preceded by at least one call to `before` in the same session. Property-shaped on purpose — asserts an ordering constraint, not an exact sequence (PRD §12.6 honesty clause).
